{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_78c90f38fd25423c9ebc16d3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage validate\n2\t\n3\t/*\n4\tPackage validate provides a standardized, extensible validation error format for HTTP API testing.\n5\t\n6\tThis package defines the ValidationError data structure, which offers a comprehensive, machine-readable\n7\tformat for representing validation failures across different types of API validations (HTTP status codes,\n8\terror message patterns, content types, response structures, CORS headers, and more).\n9\t\n10\t# Validation Error Format\n11\t\n12\tThe ValidationError struct is the core of this package's error reporting system. It follows these design\n13\tprinciples:\n14\t\n15\t  - Serializable: Full JSON marshaling/unmarshaling support via struct tags\n16\t  - Machine-readable: Clear field names suitable for automated processing and log aggregation\n17\t  - Human-readable: Descriptive messages with helpful suggestions for debugging\n18\t  - Extensible: Optional fields provide additional context without breaking existing code\n19\t\n20\t# Required vs Optional Fields\n21\t\n22\tRequired fields that must be set for every ValidationError:\n23\t  - ErrorType: The validation category (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n24\t  - Message: A concise, human-readable description of the validation failure\n25\t\n26\tOptional fields that provide additional context:\n27\t  - Expected/Actual: The values that were expected and actually received\n28\t  - Context: Where/when the validation occurred (e.g., endpoint, operation type)\n29\t  - FieldName: Specific field where the error was found (e.g., in response body JSON)\n30\t  - Location: Position information (e.g., \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\")\n31\t  - RelatedFields: Other fields related to this error for additional context\n32\t  - PatternDetails: Information about regex pattern matching failures\n33\t  - RangeInfo: Range boundaries for range-based validations (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n34\t  - ValidationDetails: Additional validation-specific information as a list\n35\t  - ResponseSnippet: Truncated excerpt from the response for debugging\n36\t  - Suggestions: Actionable recommendations for resolving the error\n37\t\n38\t# Creating Validation Errors\n39\t\n40\tThis package provides several ways to create ValidationError instances:\n41\t\n42\t  1. Direct struct initialization: Create ValidationError directly for full control\n43\t  2. ValidationFormatter builder: Use the builder pattern for ergonomic, fluent construction\n44\t  3. Convenience functions: Use pre-built functions like FormatStatusCodeError for common cases\n45\t  4. Custom validation: Use FormatCustomValidationError with FormatOption for advanced scenarios\n46\t\n47\tExample usage:\n48\t\n49\t\t// Using the ValidationFormatter builder\n50\t\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n51\t\t\tWithExpected(200).\n52\t\t\tWithActual(404).\n53\t\t\tWithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n54\t\t\tFormat()\n55\t\t// Output:\n56\t\t// status_code validation failed\n57\t\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n58\t\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n59\t\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n60\t\t//   Suggestions:\n61\t\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n62\t\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n63\t\n64\t\t// Using a convenience function\n65\t\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n66\t\n67\t# Error Interface\n68\t\n69\tValidationError implements the error interface, producing detailed, multi-line error messages\n70\tthat include all relevant validation information. The Error() method formats the output\n71\twith expected/actual values, context, field information, and suggestions for resolution.\n72\t\n73\t# Serialization\n74\t\n75\tValidationError includes JSON struct tags for all fields, enabling easy serialization:\n76\t\n77\t\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n78\t\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n79\t\t// Output: {\"error_type\":\"status_code\",\"message\":\"test\"}\n80\t\n81\tThe ToMap() method provides flexible map[string]interface{} conversion for dynamic\n82\tmanipulation before serialization.\n83\t\n84\t# Validation\n85\t\n86\tThe Validate() method checks that a ValidationError is properly structured with\n87\trequired fields populated. Use this to catch programming errors early:\n88\t\n89\t\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n90\t\tif err := err.Validate(); err != nil {\n91\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"Invalid error structure: %v\", err)\n92\t\t}\n93\t*/\n94\t\n95\timport (\n96\t\t\"fmt\"\n97\t\t\"strings\"\n98\t)\n99\t\n100\t// =============================================================================\n101\t// HELPER TYPES\n102\t// =============================================================================\n103\t\n104\t// ValidationErrorData is a simplified, serialization-friendly version of ValidationError.\n105\t// It provides a consistent format for external communication and API responses.\n106\t// This type is designed to be easily marshaled to JSON and consumed by external systems.\n107\t//\n108\t// Relationship to ValidationError:\n109\t// - ValidationError is the internal, comprehensive error structure used by the validate package\n110\t// - ValidationErrorData is the external, simplified format returned to API clients\n111\t// - Use ToValidationErrorData() to convert ValidationError to ValidationErrorData\n112\t//\n113\t// # Required Fields\n114\t//\n115\t// The MessageType, ErrorType, and Message fields are required for all ValidationErrorData.\n116\t//\n117\t// # Optional Fields\n118\t//\n119\t// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the specific validation scenario.\n120\t//\n121\t// Example usage:\n122\t//\n123\t//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n124\t//\t    MessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n125\t//\t    ErrorType:    \"status_code\",\n126\t//\t    Message:      \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n127\t//\t    Context:      \"GET /api/users/123\",\n128\t//\t    Expected:     200,\n129\t//\t    Actual:       404,\n130\t//\t    Suggestions:  []string{\"Check the endpoint URL\", \"Verify resource exists\"},\n131\t//\t}\n132\t//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(data)\n133\ttype ValidationErrorData struct {\n134\t\t// MessageType is a fixed identifier for this type of error message.\n135\t\t// Always \"validation_failed\" for ValidationErrorData instances.\n136\t\t// This field allows clients to quickly identify the error category.\n137\t\tMessageType string `json:\"message_type\"`\n138\t\n139\t\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n140\t\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n141\t\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n142\t\t// Use the ErrorType* constants for type-safe error type specification.\n143\t\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n144\t\n145\t\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n146\t\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n147\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n148\t\n149\t\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n150\t\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n151\t\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n152\t\n153\t\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n154\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n155\t\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n156\t\n157\t\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n158\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n159\t\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n160\t\n161\t\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n162\t\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n163\t\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n164\t\n165\t\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n166\t\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n167\t\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n168\t\n169\t\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n170\t\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n171\t\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n172\t\n173\t\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n174\t\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n175\t\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n176\t\n177\t\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n178\t\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n179\t\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n180\t\n181\t\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n182\t\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n183\t\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n184\t\n185\t\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n186\t\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n187\t\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n188\t\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n189\t\n190\t\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n191\t\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n192\t\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// Error implements the error interface for ValidationErrorData.\n196\t// It returns the Message field for compatibility with error handling patterns.\n197\t//\n198\t// Example usage:\n199\t//\n200\t//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{Message: \"Validation failed\"}\n201\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Error: %v\", data)\n202\tfunc (ve ValidationErrorData) Error() string {\n203\t\treturn ve.Message\n204\t}\n205\t\n206\t// ToValidationErrorData converts a ValidationError to ValidationErrorData format.\n207\t// This function creates a simplified, serialization-friendly version of the validation error\n208\t// suitable for external communication and API responses.\n209\t//\n210\t// The conversion maps ValidationError fields to ValidationErrorData fields,\n211\t// with the following special handling:\n212\t// - ErrorType is directly copied\n213\t// - Message is generated if empty\n214\t// - MessageType is always set to \"validation_failed\"\n215\t// - All other fields are preserved if present\n216\t//\n217\t// Parameters:\n218\t//   - ve: The ValidationError to convert\n219\t//\n220\t// Returns a ValidationErrorData instance with populated fields.\n221\t//\n222\t// Example usage:\n223\t//\n224\t//\tve := ValidationError{\n225\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n226\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n227\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n228\t//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users\",\n229\t//\t}\n230\t//\tdata := ToValidationErrorData(ve)\n231\t//\t// data.MessageType = \"validation_failed\"\n232\t//\t// data.ErrorType = \"status_code\"\n233\t//\t// data.Context = \"GET /api/users\"\n234\tfunc ToValidationErrorData(ve ValidationError) ValidationErrorData {\n235\t\t// Generate message if not provided\n236\t\tmessage := ve.Message\n237\t\tif message == \"\" {\n238\t\t\t// Generate detailed message with expected/actual values\n239\t\t\tmessage = generateValidationMessage(ve.ErrorType, ve.Expected, ve.Actual)\n240\t\t}\n241\t\n242\t\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n243\t\t\tMessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n244\t\t\tErrorType:    ve.ErrorType,\n245\t\t\tMessage:      message,\n246\t\t\tContext:      ve.Context,\n247\t\t\tExpected:     ve.Expected,\n248\t\t\tActual:       ve.Actual,\n249\t\t\tFieldName:    ve.FieldName,\n250\t\t\tLocation:     ve.Location,\n251\t\t\tRelatedFields:     ve.RelatedFields,\n252\t\t\tPatternDetails:    ve.PatternDetails,\n253\t\t\tRangeInfo:         ve.RangeInfo,\n254\t\t\tValidationDetails: ve.ValidationDetails,\n255\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   ve.ResponseSnippet,\n256\t\t\tSuggestions:       ve.Suggestions,\n257\t\t}\n258\t\n259\t\treturn data\n260\t}\n261\t\n262\t// generateValidationMessage creates a detailed validation message with expected/actual values.\n263\tfunc generateValidationMessage(errorType string, expected, actual interface{}) string {\n264\t\tvar parts []string\n265\t\n266\t\t// Add expected value details\n267\t\tif expected != nil {\n268\t\t\tswitch exp := expected.(type) {\n269\t\t\tcase int:\n270\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n271\t\t\tcase []int:\n272\t\t\t\texpectedStr := \"one of [\"\n273\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n274\t\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n275\t\t\t\t\t\texpectedStr += \", \"\n276\t\t\t\t\t}\n277\t\t\t\t\texpectedStr += fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n278\t\t\t\t}\n279\t\t\t\texpectedStr += \"]\"\n280\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, expectedStr)\n281\t\t\tcase string:\n282\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected '%s'\", exp))\n283\t\t\tdefault:\n284\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %v\", exp))\n285\t\t\t}\n286\t\t}\n287\t\n288\t\t// Add actual value details\n289\t\tif actual != nil {\n290\t\t\tswitch act := actual.(type) {\n291\t\t\tcase int:\n292\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n293\t\t\tcase string:\n294\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 50 {\n295\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:50] + \"...\"\n296\t\t\t\t}\n297\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got '%s'\", act))\n298\t\t\tdefault:\n299\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %v\", act))\n300\t\t\t}\n301\t\t}\n302\t\n303\t\t// Combine parts\n304\t\tif len(parts) > 0 {\n305\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed: %s\", errorType, strings.Join(parts, \" \"))\n306\t\t}\n307\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", errorType)\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t// ValidationError is the core data structure for validation error representation.\n311\t// It provides a standardized format for validation errors across different\n312\t// validation types (status codes, error messages, content types, etc.).\n313\t//\n314\t// This structure is designed to be:\n315\t// - Serializable: Includes JSON tags for marshaling/unmarshaling\n316\t// - Machine-readable: Clear field names for automated processing\n317\t// - Human-readable: Descriptive messages for debugging\n318\t// - Extensible: Optional fields for additional context\n319\t//\n320\t// # Required Fields\n321\t//\n322\t// The Message and ErrorType fields are required for all validation errors.\n323\t//\n324\t// # Optional Fields\n325\t//\n326\t// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the\n327\t// specific validation scenario.\n328\t//\n329\t// Example usage:\n330\t//\n331\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n332\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code_mismatch\",\n333\t//\t    Message:   \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n334\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n335\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n336\t//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users/123\",\n337\t//\t}\n338\t//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n339\ttype ValidationError struct {\n340\t\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n341\t\t// This field identifies what kind of validation was performed and failed.\n342\t\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n343\t\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n344\t\t//\n345\t\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n346\t\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n347\t\n348\t\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n349\t\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n350\t\t// The message should be concise but informative enough for debugging.\n351\t\t//\n352\t\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n353\t\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n354\t\n355\t\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n356\t\t// This can include endpoint names, operation types, or other contextual details.\n357\t\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n358\t\t// This field is optional and can be empty if no context is available.\n359\t\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n360\t\n361\t\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n362\t\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n363\t\t// When present, it helps identify the discrepancy between expected and actual values.\n364\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n365\t\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n366\t\n367\t\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n368\t\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n369\t\t// When present, it shows the specific value that caused the validation failure.\n370\t\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n371\t\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n372\t\n373\t\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n374\t\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n375\t\t// This field is optional.\n376\t\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n377\t\n378\t\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n379\t\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n380\t\t// This field is optional.\n381\t\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n382\t\n383\t\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n384\t\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n385\t\t// This field is optional.\n386\t\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n387\t\n388\t\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n389\t\t// This is useful when validating error messages against regex patterns.\n390\t\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n391\t\t// This field is optional.\n392\t\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n393\t\n394\t\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n395\t\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n396\t\t// This field is optional.\n397\t\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n398\t\n399\t\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n400\t\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n401\t\t// This field is optional.\n402\t\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n403\t\n404\t\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n405\t\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n406\t\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n407\t\t// This field is optional.\n408\t\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n409\t\n410\t\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n411\t\t// This field is optional and contains hints or steps to fix the issue.\n412\t\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n413\t\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// Error implements the error interface for ValidationError.\n417\t// It returns a detailed, multi-line error message that includes all relevant\n418\t// validation information for debugging and resolution.\n419\t//\n420\t// The output includes:\n421\t// - The validation type\n422\t// - Expected vs actual values\n423\t// - Context (if provided)\n424\t// - Field name (if provided)\n425\t// - Location (if provided)\n426\t// - Related fields (if provided)\n427\t// - Pattern details (if provided)\n428\t// - Range info (if provided)\n429\t// - Validation details (if provided)\n430\t// - Response snippet (if provided)\n431\t// - Suggestions for fixing the issue\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n436\t//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n437\t//\t    Message:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n438\t//\t    Expected:  200,\n439\t//\t    Actual:    404,\n440\t//\t}\n441\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n442\tfunc (ve ValidationError) Error() string {\n443\t\tvar b strings.Builder\n444\t\n445\t\t// Use the Message field as the first line if it's different from the default\n446\t\tif ve.Message != \"\" && !strings.HasPrefix(ve.Message, ve.ErrorType+\" validation failed\") {\n447\t\t\tb.WriteString(ve.Message)\n448\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n449\t\t} else {\n450\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\\n\", ve.ErrorType))\n451\t\t}\n452\t\n453\t\t// Expected vs Actual\n454\t\tif ve.Expected != nil || ve.Actual != nil {\n455\t\t\t// For status code validation, use \"Expected:\" and \"Received:\" labels\n456\t\t\tisStatusCode := ve.ErrorType == \"status_code\"\n457\t\n458\t\t\tswitch exp := ve.Expected.(type) {\n459\t\t\tcase int:\n460\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n461\t\t\tcase []int:\n462\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Expected: one of [\")\n463\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n464\t\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n465\t\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\", \")\n466\t\t\t\t\t}\n467\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n468\t\t\t\t}\n469\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"]\\n\")\n470\t\t\tcase string:\n471\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %s\\n\", exp))\n472\t\t\tdefault:\n473\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %v\\n\", exp))\n474\t\t\t}\n475\t\n476\t\t\t// Actual value - use \"Received:\" for status code validation\n477\t\t\tswitch act := ve.Actual.(type) {\n478\t\t\tcase int:\n479\t\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n480\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %d (%s)\\n\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n481\t\t\t\t} else {\n482\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n483\t\t\t\t}\n484\t\t\tcase string:\n485\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n486\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n487\t\t\t\t}\n488\t\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n489\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %s\\n\", act))\n490\t\t\t\t} else {\n491\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %s\\n\", act))\n492\t\t\t\t}\n493\t\t\tdefault:\n494\t\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n495\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %v\\n\", act))\n496\t\t\t\t} else {\n497\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %v\\n\", act))\n498\t\t\t\t}\n499\t\t\t}\n500\t\t}\n501\t\n502\t\t// Context - use \"Request:\" for status code validation\n503\t\tif ve.Context != \"\" {\n504\t\t\tif ve.ErrorType == \"status_code\" {\n505\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Request:  %s\\n\", ve.Context))\n506\t\t\t} else {\n507\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", ve.Context))\n508\t\t\t}\n509\t\t}\n510\t\n511\t\t// Field name\n512\t\tif ve.FieldName != \"\" {\n513\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field:    %s\\n\", ve.FieldName))\n514\t\t}\n515\t\n516\t\t// Location\n517\t\tif ve.Location != \"\" {\n518\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: %s\\n\", ve.Location))\n519\t\t}\n520\t\n521\t\t// Related fields\n522\t\tif len(ve.RelatedFields) > 0 {\n523\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Related fields: [\")\n524\t\t\tfor i, field := range ve.RelatedFields {\n525\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n526\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\", \")\n527\t\t\t\t}\n528\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(field)\n529\t\t\t}\n530\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"]\\n\")\n531\t\t}\n532\t\n533\t\t// Pattern details\n534\t\tif ve.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n535\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Pattern:  %s\\n\", ve.PatternDetails))\n536\t\t}\n537\t\n538\t\t// Range info\n539\t\tif ve.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n540\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Range:    %s\\n\", ve.RangeInfo))\n541\t\t}\n542\t\n543\t\t// Validation details\n544\t\tif len(ve.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n545\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Details:\\n\")\n546\t\t\tfor _, detail := range ve.ValidationDetails {\n547\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    - %s\\n\", detail))\n548\t\t\t}\n549\t\t}\n550\t\n551\t\t// Response snippet\n552\t\tif ve.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n553\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Response: %s\\n\", ve.ResponseSnippet))\n554\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t// Suggestions - format as \"Common causes:\" for status code validation\n557\t\tif len(ve.Suggestions) > 0 {\n558\t\t\t// Use \"Common causes:\" for status code validation, \"Suggestions:\" otherwise\n559\t\t\tif ve.ErrorType == \"status_code\" {\n560\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Common causes:\\n\")\n561\t\t\t\tfor i, suggestion := range ve.Suggestions {\n562\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    %d. %s\\n\", i+1, suggestion))\n563\t\t\t\t}\n564\t\t\t} else {\n565\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Suggestions:\\n\")\n566\t\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range ve.Suggestions {\n567\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    - %s\\n\", suggestion))\n568\t\t\t\t}\n569\t\t\t}\n570\t\t}\n571\t\n572\t\treturn b.String()\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t// getStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code.\n576\t// This is a lightweight version for error formatting.\n577\tfunc getStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n578\t\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n579\t\t\t100: \"Continue\", 101: \"Switching Protocols\", Content\",\n582\t\t\t300: \"Multiple Choices\", 301: \"Moved Permanently\", 302: \"Found\",\n583\t\t\t303: \"See Other\", 304: \"Not Modified\", 307: \"Temporary Redirect\",\n584\t\t\t308: \"Permanent Redirect\",\n585\t\t\t400: \"Bad Request\", 401: \"Unauthorized\", 402: \"Payment Required\",\n586\t\t\t403: \"Forbidden\", 404: \"Not Found\", 405: \"Method Not Allowed\",\n587\t\t\t406: \"Not Acceptable\", 407: \"Proxy Authentication Required\",\n588\t\t\t408: \"Request Timeout\", 409: \"Conflict\", 410: \"Gone\",\n589\t\t\t411: \"Length Required\", 412: \"Precondition Failed\",\n590\t\t\t413: \"Payload Too Large\", 414: \"URI Too Long\",\n591\t\t\t415: \"Unsupported Media Type\", 416: \"Range Not Satisfiable\",\n592\t\t\t417: \"Expectation Failed\", 418: \"I'm a {\n606\t\t\treturn desc\n607\t\t}\n608\t\treturn \"Unknown\"\n609\t}\n610\t\n611\t// ToMap converts the ValidationError to a map[string]interface{} for flexible serialization.\n612\t// This is useful validate\n\n/*\nPackage validate provides a standardized, extensible validation error format for HTTP API testing.\n\nThis package defines the ValidationError data structure, which offers a comprehensive, machine-readable\nformat for representing validation failures across different types of API validations (HTTP status codes,\nerror message patterns, content types, response structures, CORS headers, and more).\n\n# Validation Error Format\n\nThe ValidationError struct is the core of this package's error reporting system. It follows these design\nprinciples:\n\n  - Serializable: Full JSON marshaling/unmarshaling support via struct tags\n  - Machine-readable: Clear field names suitable for automated processing and log aggregation\n  - Human-readable: Descriptive messages with helpful suggestions for debugging\n  - Extensible: Optional fields provide additional context without breaking existing code\n\n# Required vs Optional Fields\n\nRequired fields that must be set for every ValidationError:\n  - ErrorType: The validation category (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n  - Message: A concise, human-readable description of the validation failure\n\nOptional fields that provide additional context:\n  - Expected/Actual: The values that were expected and actually received\n  - Context: Where/when the validation occurred (e.g., endpoint, operation type)\n  - FieldName: Specific field where the error was found (e.g., in response body JSON)\n  - Location: Position information (e.g., \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\")\n  - RelatedFields: Other fields related to this error for additional context\n  - PatternDetails: Information about regex pattern matching failures\n  - RangeInfo: Range boundaries for range-based validations (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n  - ValidationDetails: Additional validation-specific information as a list\n  - ResponseSnippet: Truncated excerpt from the response for debugging\n  - Suggestions: Actionable recommendations for resolving the error\n\n# Creating Validation Errors\n\nThis package provides several ways to create ValidationError instances:\n\n  1. Direct struct initialization: Create ValidationError directly for full control\n  2. ValidationFormatter builder: Use the builder pattern for ergonomic, fluent construction\n  3. Convenience functions: Use pre-built functions like FormatStatusCodeError for common cases\n  4. Custom validation: Use FormatCustomValidationError with FormatOption for advanced scenarios\n\nExample usage:\n\n\t// Using the ValidationFormatter builder\n\terr := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(200).\n\t\tWithActual(404).\n\t\tWithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n\t\tFormat()\n\t// Output:\n\t// status_code validation failed\n\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n\t//   Suggestions:\n\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n\n\t// Using a convenience function\n\terr := FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n\n# Error Interface\n\nValidationError implements the error interface, producing detailed, multi-line error messages\nthat include all relevant validation information. The Error() method formats the output\nwith expected/actual values, context, field information, and suggestions for resolution.\n\n# Serialization\n\nValidationError includes JSON struct tags for all fields, enabling easy serialization:\n\n\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\n\t// Output: {\"error_type\":\"status_code\",\"message\":\"test\"}\n\nThe ToMap() method provides flexible map[string]interface{} conversion for dynamic\nmanipulation before serialization.\n\n# Validation\n\nThe Validate() method checks that a ValidationError is properly structured with\nrequired fields populated. Use this to catch programming errors early:\n\n\terr := ValidationError{ErrorType: \"status_code\", Message: \"test\"}\n\tif err := err.Validate(); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"Invalid error structure: %v\", err)\n\t}\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidationErrorData is a simplified, serialization-friendly version of ValidationError.\n// It provides a consistent format for external communication and API responses.\n// This type is designed to be easily marshaled to JSON and consumed by external systems.\n//\n// Relationship to ValidationError:\n// - ValidationError is the internal, comprehensive error structure used by the validate package\n// - ValidationErrorData is the external, simplified format returned to API clients\n// - Use ToValidationErrorData() to convert ValidationError to ValidationErrorData\n//\n// # Required Fields\n//\n// The MessageType, ErrorType, and Message fields are required for all ValidationErrorData.\n//\n// # Optional Fields\n//\n// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the specific validation scenario.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n//\t    MessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n//\t    ErrorType:    \"status_code\",\n//\t    Message:      \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n//\t    Context:      \"GET /api/users/123\",\n//\t    Expected:     200,\n//\t    Actual:       404,\n//\t    Suggestions:  []string{\"Check the endpoint URL\", \"Verify resource exists\"},\n//\t}\n//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(data)\ntype ValidationErrorData struct {\n\t// MessageType is a fixed identifier for this type of error message.\n\t// Always \"validation_failed\" for ValidationErrorData instances.\n\t// This field allows clients to quickly identify the error category.\n\tMessageType string `json:\"message_type\"`\n\n\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n\t// Use the ErrorType* constants for type-safe error type specification.\n\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n\n\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n\n\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface for ValidationErrorData.\n// It returns the Message field for compatibility with error handling patterns.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tdata := ValidationErrorData{Message: \"Validation failed\"}\n//\tlog.Printf(\"Error: %v\", data)\nfunc (ve ValidationErrorData) Error() string {\n\treturn ve.Message\n}\n\n// ToValidationErrorData converts a ValidationError to ValidationErrorData format.\n// This function creates a simplified, serialization-friendly version of the validation error\n// suitable for external communication and API responses.\n//\n// The conversion maps ValidationError fields to ValidationErrorData fields,\n// with the following special handling:\n// - ErrorType is directly copied\n// - Message is generated if empty\n// - MessageType is always set to \"validation_failed\"\n// - All other fields are preserved if present\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - ve: The ValidationError to convert\n//\n// Returns a ValidationErrorData instance with populated fields.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tve := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n//\t    Expected:  200,\n//\t    Actual:    404,\n//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users\",\n//\t}\n//\tdata := ToValidationErrorData(ve)\n//\t// data.MessageType = \"validation_failed\"\n//\t// data.ErrorType = \"status_code\"\n//\t// data.Context = \"GET /api/users\"\nfunc ToValidationErrorData(ve ValidationError) ValidationErrorData {\n\t// Generate message if not provided\n\tmessage := ve.Message\n\tif message == \"\" {\n\t\t// Generate detailed message with expected/actual values\n\t\tmessage = generateValidationMessage(ve.ErrorType, ve.Expected, ve.Actual)\n\t}\n\n\tdata := ValidationErrorData{\n\t\tMessageType:  \"validation_failed\",\n\t\tErrorType:    ve.ErrorType,\n\t\tMessage:      message,\n\t\tContext:      ve.Context,\n\t\tExpected:     ve.Expected,\n\t\tActual:       ve.Actual,\n\t\tFieldName:    ve.FieldName,\n\t\tLocation:     ve.Location,\n\t\tRelatedFields:     ve.RelatedFields,\n\t\tPatternDetails:    ve.PatternDetails,\n\t\tRangeInfo:         ve.RangeInfo,\n\t\tValidationDetails: ve.ValidationDetails,\n\t\tResponseSnippet:   ve.ResponseSnippet,\n\t\tSuggestions:       ve.Suggestions,\n\t}\n\n\treturn data\n}\n\n// generateValidationMessage creates a detailed validation message with expected/actual values.\nfunc generateValidationMessage(errorType string, expected, actual interface{}) string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\n\t// Add expected value details\n\tif expected != nil {\n\t\tswitch exp := expected.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n\t\tcase []int:\n\t\t\texpectedStr := \"one of [\"\n\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\texpectedStr += \", \"\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\texpectedStr += fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\texpectedStr += \"]\"\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, expectedStr)\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected '%s'\", exp))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected %v\", exp))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add actual value details\n\tif actual != nil {\n\t\tswitch act := actual.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tif len(act) > 50 {\n\t\t\t\tact = act[:50] + \"...\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got '%s'\", act))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"but got %v\", act))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine parts\n\tif len(parts) > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed: %s\", errorType, strings.Join(parts, \" \"))\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", errorType)\n}\n\n// ValidationError is the core data structure for validation error representation.\n// It provides a standardized format for validation errors across different\n// validation types (status codes, error messages, content types, etc.).\n//\n// This structure is designed to be:\n// - Serializable: Includes JSON tags for marshaling/unmarshaling\n// - Machine-readable: Clear field names for automated processing\n// - Human-readable: Descriptive messages for debugging\n// - Extensible: Optional fields for additional context\n//\n// # Required Fields\n//\n// The Message and ErrorType fields are required for all validation errors.\n//\n// # Optional Fields\n//\n// All other fields are optional and should be populated based on the\n// specific validation scenario.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code_mismatch\",\n//\t    Message:   \"Expected status code 200 but got 404\",\n//\t    Expected:  200,\n//\t    Actual:    404,\n//\t    Context:   \"GET /api/users/123\",\n//\t}\n//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(err)\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\t// ErrorType is the specific validation category that failed.\n\t// This field identifies what kind of validation was performed and failed.\n\t// Common values include: \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\",\n\t// \"status_code_range\", \"cors_headers\", \"response_structure\".\n\t//\n\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n\tErrorType string `json:\"error_type\"`\n\n\t// Message is a human-readable description of what went wrong.\n\t// This should be a clear, actionable message that explains the validation failure.\n\t// The message should be concise but informative enough for debugging.\n\t//\n\t// This field is required for all validation errors.\n\tMessage string `json:\"message\"`\n\n\t// Context provides additional information about where or when the validation occurred.\n\t// This can include endpoint names, operation types, or other contextual details.\n\t// Examples: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"OAuth token validation\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n\t// This field is optional and can be empty if no context is available.\n\tContext string `json:\"context,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Expected contains the value that was expected during validation.\n\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n\t// When present, it helps identify the discrepancy between expected and actual values.\n\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n\tExpected interface{} `json:\"expected,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Actual contains the value that was actually received during validation.\n\t// This field is optional and may not be relevant for all validation types.\n\t// When present, it shows the specific value that caused the validation failure.\n\t// Can be of any type: int, string, []int, etc.\n\tActual interface{} `json:\"actual,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// FieldName specifies the field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\").\n\t// This is particularly useful for error message validation in response bodies.\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tFieldName string `json:\"field_name,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Location specifies where in the input the error occurred.\n\t// Examples: \"line 5\", \"field 'user.email'\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\"\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tLocation string `json:\"location,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RelatedFields lists fields related to this error for additional context.\n\t// Examples: [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"], [\"access_token\", \"refresh_token\"]\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tRelatedFields []string `json:\"related_fields,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// PatternDetails contains information about pattern matching failures.\n\t// This is useful when validating error messages against regex patterns.\n\t// Example: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tPatternDetails string `json:\"pattern_details,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// RangeInfo specifies range boundaries for range validation failures.\n\t// Example: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tRangeInfo string `json:\"range_info,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ValidationDetails contains additional validation-specific information.\n\t// This can include multiple details as a list of strings for comprehensive error reporting.\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tValidationDetails []string `json:\"validation_details,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// ResponseSnippet is a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n\t// This should be limited to a reasonable length (e.g., 200 characters) to keep\n\t// error messages readable while providing useful context.\n\t// This field is optional.\n\tResponseSnippet string `json:\"response_snippet,omitempty\"`\n\n\t// Suggestions provides actionable recommendations for resolving the validation error.\n\t// This field is optional and contains hints or steps to fix the issue.\n\t// When provided, suggestions should be specific and actionable.\n\tSuggestions []string `json:\"suggestions,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface for ValidationError.\n// It returns a detailed, multi-line error message that includes all relevant\n// validation information for debugging and resolution.\n//\n// The output includes:\n// - The validation type\n// - Expected vs actual values\n// - Context (if provided)\n// - Field name (if provided)\n// - Location (if provided)\n// - Related fields (if provided)\n// - Pattern details (if provided)\n// - Range info (if provided)\n// - Validation details (if provided)\n// - Response snippet (if provided)\n// - Suggestions for fixing the issue\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n//\t    Message:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n//\t    Expected:  200,\n//\t    Actual:    404,\n//\t}\n//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\nfunc (ve ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tvar b strings.Builder\n\n\t// Use the Message field as the first line if it's different from the default\n\tif ve.Message != \"\" && !strings.HasPrefix(ve.Message, ve.ErrorType+\" validation failed\") {\n\t\tb.WriteString(ve.Message)\n\t\tb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t} else {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\\n\", ve.ErrorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Expected vs Actual\n\tif ve.Expected != nil || ve.Actual != nil {\n\t\t// For status code validation, use \"Expected:\" and \"Received:\" labels\n\t\tisStatusCode := ve.ErrorType == \"status_code\"\n\n\t\tswitch exp := ve.Expected.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %d (%s)\\n\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp)))\n\t\tcase []int:\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Expected: one of [\")\n\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n\t\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code)))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"]\\n\")\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %s\\n\", exp))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected: %v\\n\", exp))\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Actual value - use \"Received:\" for status code validation\n\t\tswitch act := ve.Actual.(type) {\n\t\tcase int:\n\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %d (%s)\\n\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %d (%s)\\n\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act)))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase string:\n\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %s\\n\", act))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %s\\n\", act))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tif isStatusCode {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Received: %v\\n\", act))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual:   %v\\n\", act))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Context - use \"Request:\" for status code validation\n\tif ve.Context != \"\" {\n\t\tif ve.ErrorType == \"status_code\" {\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Request:  %s\\n\", ve.Context))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context:  %s\\n\", ve.Context))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Field name\n\tif ve.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field:    %s\\n\", ve.FieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Location\n\tif ve.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: %s\\n\", ve.Location))\n\t}\n\n\t// Related fields\n\tif len(ve.RelatedFields) > 0 {\n\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Related fields: [\")\n\t\tfor i, field := range ve.RelatedFields {\n\t\t\tif i > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(field)\n\t\t}\n\t\tb.WriteString(\"]\\n\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern details\n\tif ve.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Pattern:  %s\\n\", ve.PatternDetails))\n\t}\n\n\t// Range info\n\tif ve.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Range:    %s\\n\", ve.RangeInfo))\n\t}\n\n\t// Validation details\n\tif len(ve.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Details:\\n\")\n\t\tfor _, detail := range ve.ValidationDetails {\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    - %s\\n\", detail))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Response snippet\n\tif ve.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Response: %s\\n\", ve.ResponseSnippet))\n\t}\n\n\t// Suggestions - format as \"Common causes:\" for status code validation\n\tif len(ve.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\t// Use \"Common causes:\" for status code validation, \"Suggestions:\" otherwise\n\t\tif ve.ErrorType == \"status_code\" {\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Common causes:\\n\")\n\t\t\tfor i, suggestion := range ve.Suggestions {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    %d. %s\\n\", i+1, suggestion))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tb.WriteString(\"  Suggestions:\\n\")\n\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range ve.Suggestions {\n\t\t\t\tb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    - %s\\n\", suggestion))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn b.String()\n}\n\n// getStatusCodeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code.\n// This is a lightweight version for error formatting.\nfunc getStatusCodeDescription(code int) string {\n\tdescriptions := map[int]string{\n\t\t100: \"Continue\", 101: \"Switching Protocols\", 102: \"Processing\",\n\t\t200: \"OK\", 201: \"Created\", 202: \"Accepted\", 203: \"Non-Authoritative Information\",\n\t\t204: \"No Content\", 205: \"Reset Content\", 206: \"Partial Content\",\n\t\t300: \"Multiple Choices\", 301: \"Moved Permanently\", 302: \"Found\",\n\t\t303: \"See Other\", 304: \"Not Modified\", 307: \"Temporary Redirect\",\n\t\t308: \"Permanent Redirect\",\n\t\t400: \"Bad Request\", 401: \"Unauthorized\", 402: \"Payment Required\",\n\t\t403: \"Forbidden\", 404: \"Not Found\", 405: \"Method Not Allowed\",\n\t\t406: \"Not Acceptable\", 407: \"Proxy Authentication Required\",\n\t\t408: \"Request Timeout\", 409: \"Conflict\", 410: \"Gone\",\n\t\t411: \"Length Required\", 412: \"Precondition Failed\",\n\t\t413: \"Payload Too Large\", 414: \"URI Too Long\",\n\t\t415: \"Unsupported Media Type\", 416: \"Range Not Satisfiable\",\n\t\t417: \"Expectation Failed\", 418: \"I'm a teapot\",\n\t\t422: \"Unprocessable Entity\", 423: \"Locked\", 424: \"Failed Dependency\",\n\t\t426: \"Upgrade Required\", 428: \"Precondition Required\",\n\t\t429: \"Too Many Requests\", 431: \"Request Header Fields Too Large\",\n\t\t451: \"Unavailable For Legal Reasons\",\n\t\t500: \"Internal Server Error\", 501: \"Not Implemented\",\n\t\t502: \"Bad Gateway\", 503: \"Service Unavailable\",\n\t\t504: \"Gateway Timeout\", 505: \"HTTP Version Not Supported\",\n\t\t506: \"Variant Also Negotiates\", 507: \"Insufficient Storage\",\n\t\t508: \"Loop Detected\", 510: \"Not Extended\",\n\t\t511: \"Network Authentication Required\",\n\t}\n\n\tif desc, ok := descriptions[code]; ok {\n\t\treturn desc\n\t}\n\treturn \"Unknown\"\n}\n\n// ToMap converts the ValidationError to a map[string]interface{} for flexible serialization.\n// This is useful when you need to dynamically manipulate or filter the error data before\n// JSON encoding or when working with systems that require map input.\n//\n// Returns a map containing all non-empty fields from the ValidationError.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n//\t    Message:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n//\t    Expected:  200,\n//\t    Actual:    404,\n//\t}\n//\tdata := err.ToMap()\n//\tjsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(data)\nfunc (ve ve.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tresult[\"field_name\"] = ve.FieldName\n\t}\n\tif ve.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tresult[\"location\"] = ve.Location\n\t}\n\tif len(ve.RelatedFields) > 0 {\n\t\tresult[\"related_fields\"] = ve.RelatedFields\n\t}\n\tif ve.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n\t\tresult[\"pattern_details\"] = ve.PatternDetails\n\t}\n\tif ve.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n\t\tresult[\"range_info\"] = ve.RangeInfo\n\t}\n\tif len(ve.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n\t\tresult[\"validation_details\"] = ve.ValidationDetails\n\t}\n\tif ve.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n\t\tresult[\"response_snippet\"] = ve.ResponseSnippet\n\t}\n\tif len(ve.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tresult[\"suggestions\"] = ve.Suggestions\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// Validate checks if the ValidationError is valid for use.\n// A valid ValidationError must have both ErrorType and Message fields populated.\n//\n// Returns an error if the ValidationError is missing required fields, nil structure: %v\", err)\n//\t}\nfunc (ve ValidationError) Validate() error {\n\tif ve.ErrorType == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ValidationError must have ErrorType field set\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.Message _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Create validation error\n\t\t\tve := FormatValidationError(\"status_code\", tt.expected, tt.actual, \"\", \"\")\n\n\t\t\t// !strings.Contains(errMsg, keyword) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected suggestions to contain '%s' for status code %d\\nGot: %s\", keyword, tt.actual, errMsg)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_ErrorCategories verifies that error categories\n// are correctly identified in detailed validation results.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_ErrorCategories(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\tactual        int\n\t\tisClientError bool\n\t\tisServerError bool\n\t\tcategory      string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:          \"200 is success\",\n\t\t\tactual:        200,\n\t\t\tisClientError: false,\n\t\t\tisServerError: false,\n\t\t\tcategory:      \"success\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:          \"404 is client informational\",\n\t\t\tactual:        100,\n\t\t\tisClientError: false,\n\t\t\tisServerError: false,\n\t\t\tcategory:      \"other\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Create a response with the actual status code\n\t\t\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\t\t\tresp.Code = tt.actual\n\t\t\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t\t\t// Validate with details\n\t\t\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, 999) // Use invalid expected code to ensure mismatch\n\n\t\t\t// Verify category classification\n\t\t\tif result.IsClientError != tt.isClientError {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected IsClientError to be %v for status %d, got %v\", tt.isClientError, tt.actual, result.IsClientError)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif result.IsServerError != tt.isServerError {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected IsServerError to be %v for status %d, got %v\", tt.isServerError, tt.actual, result.IsServerError)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif result.Category != tt.category {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Category to be '%s' for status %d, got '%s'\", tt.category, tt.actual, result.Category)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_ResponseSnippet verifies that response snippets\n// are properly truncated and sanitized in error messages.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_ResponseSnippet(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname            string\n\t\tresponse        string\n\t\tsnippetContains string\n\t\tsnippetOmits    string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"short response included fully\",\n\t\t\tresponse:        `{\"error\": \"not found\"}`,\n\t\t\tsnippetContains: `{\"error\": \"not found\"}`,\n\t\t\tsnippetOmits:    \"\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"long response is truncated\",\n\t\t\tresponse:        `{\"error\": \"` + strings.Repeat(\"a\", 300) + `\"}`,\n\t\t\tsnippetContains: \"...\",\n\t\t\tsnippetOmits:    \"\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"newlines are sanitized\",\n\t\t\tresponse:        \"{\\n  \\\"error\\\": \\\"test\\\"\\n}\\n\",\n\t\t\tsnippetContains: \"{\",\n\t\t\tsnippetOmits:    \"\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"carriage returns are sanitized\",\n\t\t\tresponse:        \"{\\r  \\\"error\\\": \\\"test\\\"\\r}\\r\",\n\t\t\tsnippetContains: \"{\",\n\t\t\tsnippetOmits:    \"\\r\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Validate error message\n\t\t\terr := ValidateErrorMessage([]byte(tt.response), \"xyz\")\n\n\t\t\t// Verify an error is returned\n\t\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error for response: %s\", tt.response)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Convert error to string for content checking\n\t\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\n\t\t\t// Check for required content\n\t\t\tif tt.snippetContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(errMsg, tt.snippetContains) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got: %s\", tt.snippetContains, errMsg)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check that sanitized content is omitted\n\t\t\tif tt.snippetOmits != \"\" && strings.Contains(errMsg, tt.snippetOmits) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to omit '%s', got: %s\", tt.snippetOmits, errMsg)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_DetailedResultFields verifies that all fields\n// in detailed validation results are correctly populated.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_DetailedResultFields(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Create a response with status 404\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 404\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with multiple expected codes\n\texpectedCodes := []int{200, 201, 204}\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, expectedCodes)\n\n\t// Verify all result fields are correctly populated\n\tif result.Valid {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected validation to fail\")\n\t}\n\n\tif result.ActualCode != 404 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected ActualCode to be 404, got %d\", result.ActualCode)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(result.ExpectedCodes) != len(expectedCodes) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %d ExpectedCodes, got %d\", len(expectedCodes), len(result.ExpectedCodes))\n\t}\n\n\tfor i, code := range expectedCodes {\n\t\tif result.ExpectedCodes[i] != code {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected ExpectedCodes[%d] to be %d, got %d\", i, code, result.ExpectedCodes[i])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif result.MatchedCode != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected MatchedCode to be nil for failed validation, got %v\", *result.MatchedCode)\n\t}\n\n\tif result.MismatchDetails == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected MismatchDetails to be populated\")\n\t}\n\n\tif !result.IsClientError {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected IsClientError to be true for 404\")\n\t}\n\n\tif result.IsServerError {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected IsServerError to be false for 404\")\n\t}\n\n\tif result.Category != \"client_error\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Category to be 'client_error', got '%s'\", result.Category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_ErrorMessageValidationResult verifies that\n// error message validation results include all required fields.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_ErrorMessageValidationResult(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname              string\n\t\tresponse          interface{}\n\t\tpattern           EnhancedErrorMessagePattern\n\t\twantValid         bool\n\t\twantFound         bool\n\t\twantPatternMatched bool\n\t\tmustContainIssues []string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"successful validation with pattern match\",\n\t\t\tresponse: map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"invalid token\"},\n\t\t\tpattern: EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n\t\t\t\tPattern: \"invalid.*token\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantValid:         true,\n\t\t\twantFound:         true,\n\t\t\twantPatternMatched: true,\n\t\t\tmustContainIssues:  nil,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"failed \"invalid.*token\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantValid:         false,\n\t\t\twantFound:         true,\n\t\t\twantPatternMatched: false,\n\t\t\tmustContainIssues: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"pattern\",\n\t\t\t\t\"invalid.*token\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"failed validation - must contain missing\",\n\t\t\tresponse: map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"something\"},\n\t\t\tpattern: EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n\t\t\t\tMustContain: []string{\"invalid\", \"token\"},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantValid:         false,\n\t\t\twantFound:         true,\n\t\t\twantPatternMatched: true,\n\t\t\tmustContainIssues: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"invalid\",\n\t\t\t\t\"token\",\n\t\t\t\t\"required\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"failed validation - length too short\",\n\t\t\tresponse: map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"short\"},\n\t\t\tpattern: EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n\t\t\t\tMinLength: 10,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantValid:         false,\n\t\t\twantFound:         true,\n\t\t\twantPatternMatched: true,\n\t\t\tmustContainIssues: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"length\",\n\t\t\t\t\"minimum\",\n\t\t\t\t\"10\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"failed validation - contains forbidden string\",\n\t\t\tresponse: map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"invalid password\"},\n\t\t\tpattern: EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n\t\t\t\tMustNotContain: []string{\"password\", \"secret\"},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantValid:         false,\n\t\t\twantFound:         true,\n\t\t\twantPatternMatched: true,\n\t\t\tmustContainIssues: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"password\",\n\t\t\t\t\"forbidden\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(tt.response, tt.pattern)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify basic validation results\n\t\t\tif result.Valid != tt.wantValid {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Valid to be %v, got %v\", tt.wantValid, result.Valid)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif result.Found != tt.wantFound {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Found to be %v, got %v\", tt.wantFound, result.Found)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif result.PatternMatched != tt.wantPatternMatched {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected PatternMatched to be %v, got %v\", tt.wantPatternMatched, result.PatternMatched)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify issues contain expected content\n\t\t\tif tt.mustContainIssues != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif len(result.Issues) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected Issues to be populated for failed validation\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tfor _, expectedIssue := range tt.mustContainIssues {\n\t\t\t\t\tfound := false\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, issue := range result.Issues {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif strings.Contains(issue, expectedIssue) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected Issues to contain '%s', got: %v\", expectedIssue, result.Issues)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// Example demonstrating how to use detailed validation error information\nfunc ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_errorContent() {\n\t// Create a response with status 404\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 404\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with expected status 200 (no context)\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, 200)\n\n\t// Use detailed error information\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Validation failed:\\n%s\\n\", result.MismatchDetails)\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Category: %s\\n\", result.Category)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Validation failed:\n\t// Status code category: 4xx - Client error responses\n\t// Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t// Received: 404 (Not Found)\n\t// Common causes:\n\t//   1. Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n\t//   2. Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n\t//   3. Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n\t//   4. Review API versioning (URL path may have changed)\n\t// Category: client_error\n}\n\n// ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_withContext demonstrates validation with request context\nfunc ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_withContext() {\n\t// Create a response with status 401\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 401\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with expected status 200 and provide request context\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, 200, \"POST /api/users\")\n\n\t// Use detailed error information\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Validation failed:\\n%s\\n\", result.MismatchDetails)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Validation failed:\n\t// Request: POST /api/users\n\t// Status code category: 4xx - Client error responses\n\t// Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t// Received: 401 (Unauthorized)\n\t// Common causes:\n\t//   1. Verify authentication credentials are correct\n\t//   2. Check if API token or session has expired\n\t//   3. Ensure Authorization header is properly formatted (e.g., 'Bearer <token>')\n\t//   4. Confirm authentication method is supported\n}\n\n// ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_rateLimit demonstrates 429 rate limit error with suggestions\nfunc ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_rateLimit() {\n\t// Create a response with status 429\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 429\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with expected status 200\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, 200, \"GET /api/data\")\n\n\t// Use detailed error information\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Validation failed:\\n%s\\n\", result.MismatchDetails)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Validation failed:\n\t// Request: GET /api/data\n\t// Status code category: 4xx - Client error responses\n\t// Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t// Received: 429 (Too Many Requests)\n\t// Common causes:\n\t//   1. Implement rate limiting and exponential backoff\n\t//   2. Check API quota limits and current usage\n\t//   3. Consider caching responses to reduce request frequency\n\t//   4. Review rate limit headers (Retry-After, X-RateLimit-Remaining)\n}\n\n// ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_serverError demonstrates 500 server error with suggestions\nfunc ExampleValidateStatusCodeWithDetails_serverError() {\n\t// Create a response with status 500\n\tresp := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tresp.Code = 500\n\thttpResp := resp.Result()\n\n\t// Validate with expected status 200\n\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(httpResp, 200, \"DELETE /api/resource/123\")\n\n\t// Use detailed error information\n\tif !result.Valid {\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Validation failed:\\n%s\\n\", result.MismatchDetails)\n\t\tfmt.Printf(\"Is server error: %v\\n\", result.IsServerError)\n\t}\n\n\t// Output:\n\t// Validation failed:\n\t// Request: DELETE /api/resource/123\n\t// Status code category: 5xx - Server error responses\n\t// Expected: 200 (OK)\n\t// Received: 500 (Internal Server Error)\n\t// Common causes:\n\t//   1. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff\n\t//   2. Check service status page for ongoing issues\n\t//   3. Contact support if the issue persists\n\t//   4. Verify request doesn't trigger server-side bugs\n\t// Is server error: true\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_ExtendedFields verifies that extended ValidationError\n// fields are properly populated and displayed in error messages.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_ExtendedFields(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname              string\n\t\tvalidationType   string\n\t\texpected          interface{}\n\t\tactual            interface{}\n\t\tcontext           string\n\t\tresponseSnippet   string\n\t\tfieldName         string\n\t\tlocation          string\n\t\trelatedFields     []string\n\t\tpatternDetails    string\n\t\trangeInfo         string\n\t\tvalidationDetails []string\n\t\tmustContain       []string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"error message with field nil,\n\t\t\tpatternDetails:  \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\",\n\t\t\trangeInfo:       \"\",\n\t\t\tvalidationDetails: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"No matching error field found\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Checked 3 error message fields\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tmustContain: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"error_message validation failed\",\n\t\t\t\t\"invalid.*token\",\n\t\t\t\t\"access denied\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Field:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"error\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Pattern:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"regex pattern\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Details:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"No matching error field (400-499)\",\n\t\t\tactual:          200,\n\t\t\tcontext:         \"error response validation\",\n\t\t\tresponseSnippet: \"\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:       \"\",\n\t\t\tlocation:        \"\",\n\t\t\trelatedFields:   nil,\n\t\t\tpatternDetails:  \"\",\n\t\t\trangeInfo:       \"Range: 400-499 (Client Error)\",\n\t\t\tvalidationDetails: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"Status code 200 is outside range\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:            \"comprehensive error with all fields\",\n\t\t\tvalidationType:  \"error_message\",\n\t\t\texpected:        \"not found\",\n\t\t\tactual:          \"internal server error\",\n\t\t\tcontext:         \"resource access validation\",\n\t\t\tresponseSnippet: `{\"error\": \"internal server error\"}`,\n\t\t\tfieldName:       \"error\",\n\t\t\tlocation:        \"response body\",\n\t\t\trelatedFields:   []string{\"error\", \"message\"},\n\t\t\tpatternDetails:  \"substring 'not found' not found in message\",\n\t\t\trangeInfo:       \"\",\n\t\t\tvalidationDetails: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"Expected pattern did not match\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Message length: 20 characters\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tmustContain: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"error_message validation failed\",\n\t\t\t\t\"not found\",\n\t\t\t\t\"internal server error\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Field:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"error\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Pattern:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"substring 'not found'\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Context:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"resource access validation\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Details:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Expected pattern did not match\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Response:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"internal server error\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.validationType,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.expected,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.actual,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.context,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.responseSnippet,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.fieldName,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.location,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.relatedFields,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.patternDetails,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.rangeInfo,\n\t\t\t\t\ttt.validationDetails,\n\t\t\t\t)\n\n\t\t\terrMsg := ve.Error()\n\n\t\t\t// Verify all required content is present\n\t\t\tfor _, required := range tt.mustContain {\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(errMsg, required) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s'\\nGot: %s\", required, errMsg)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Verify field-specific values are correctly set\n\t\t\tif ve.FieldName != tt.fieldName {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected FieldName to be '%s', got '%s'\", tt.fieldName, ve.FieldName)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif ve.PatternDetails != tt.patternDetails {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected PatternDetails to be '%s', got '%s'\", tt.patternDetails, ve.PatternDetails)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif ve.RangeInfo != tt.rangeInfo {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected RangeInfo to be '%s', got '%s'\", tt.rangeInfo, ve.RangeInfo)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif len(ve.ValidationDetails) != len(tt.validationDetails) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected %d ValidationDetails, got %d\", len(tt.validationDetails), len(ve.ValidationDetails))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestValidationError_Content_EnhancedErrorMessageValidation verifies that\n// ValidateErrorMessage provides detailed context including field names and pattern details.\nfunc TestValidationError_Content_EnhancedErrorMessageValidation(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname           string\n\t\tresponse       string\n\t\texpectedPattern string\n\t\tmustContain    []string\n\t\tmustNotContain []string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:           \"empty response with detailed error\",\n\t\t\tresponse:       \"\",\n\t\t\texpectedPattern: \"not found\",\n\t\t\tmustContain: []string{\n\t\t\t\t\"error_message validation failed\",\n\t\t\t\t\"non-empty response\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Details:\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Response charset=utf-8\".\n104\t//\n105\t// Parameters:\n106\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n107\t//   - expected: The expected content-type pattern (e.g., \"application/json\")\n108\t//\n109\t//
